document_text: In 2024, BCG advised a higher education consortium on deploying AI-supported academic planning tools across universities in the Middle East. The initiative aimed to improve course scheduling efficiency and optimize classroom resource allocation. During the first semester of implementation, participating universities reduced scheduling conflicts by 27% and improved classroom utilization rates by 19%. The deployment integrated academic planning systems with student enrollment databases covering approximately 410,000 students. More than 2,100 faculty administrators and operations staff completed onboarding sessions related to workflow automation and scheduling analytics. According to BCG's program assessment, universities also reduced manual timetable adjustment requests by 33%. Institutional leadership teams reported improved planning visibility and faster scheduling coordination across multiple academic departments during high-enrollment periods.
question: How did the academic planning deployment improve university operations?
ai_answer: The deployment reduced scheduling conflicts by 27%, improved classroom utilization rates by 19%, and integrated systems covering approximately 510,000 students.
explanation: The document states that the deployment covered approximately 410,000 students, not 510,000 students. The scheduling conflict reduction and classroom utilization improvements are correctly supported.
